title: uva-ursi stanfordiana
intro: Uva-ursi stanfordiana, also known as bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds, particularly arbutin, a glycosidic compound with antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of uva-ursi stanfordiana include the potential treatment of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Uva-ursi stanfordiana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
